title: 10 best herbal capsules for hyperacidity

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with hyperacidity because they contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn and bloating. Additionally, ginger has a natural calming effect on the stomach lining, reducing acid production and promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria. This helps to alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, allowing for more comfortable digestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hyperacidity.

Antacid properties: The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol, have been shown to inhibit the production of acid in the stomach, thereby helping to neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.

Gastroprotective effects: Ginger has been found to possess gastroprotective effects by reducing the formation of ulcers and protecting the mucous lining of the stomach from damage caused by excessive acid secretion, which can occur with hyperacidity.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of hyperacidity like heartburn and bloating.
Shogaols: Shogaols have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.

Volatile oils (especially gingerene and bisabolol): These essential oils have natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and infection in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate hyperacidity symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain active compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
Barks: The barks of the ginger plant have been traditionally used to treat various digestive issues, including hyperacidity, due to their antacid properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried zingiber officinale rhizome and clean it thoroughly with a soft brush.
Dry the cleaned zingiber officinale in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 100 empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the zingiber officinale powder each.
Label and date the filled capsules and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with hyperacidity because they contain allicin, a natural compound that has potent antibacterial and antifungal properties. When taken as a supplement, allicin helps to neutralize excess acid production in the stomach, reducing symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, garlic's natural antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, which can contribute to acid reflux and indigestion. As a result, herbal garlic capsules can provide effective relief from hyperacidity and promote digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Allicin: Garlic's active compound allicin has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria in the stomach that contribute to acid reflux.

Mucilages: Herbal garlic capsules often contain mucilage-rich herbs like marshmallow or slippery elm, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hyperacidity.

Antioxidants: Garlic is rich in antioxidants, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, potentially reducing acid production in the stomach.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce stomach acid production and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
S-Allyl cysteine sulfoxide (Alliin): Alliin, a precursor to allicin, has been shown to exhibit gastroprotective effects by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of stomach acid.

Sulfur compounds (especially methyl allyl disulfide and diallyl disulfide): These sulfur compounds have been found to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the stomach, which can help reduce spasms and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules for hyperacidity because they contain compounds like allicin that help reduce stomach acid and alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for hyperacidity because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for hyperacidity because they contain antioxidants and other compounds that help protect the stomach lining and prevent acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried allium sativum roots with minimal moisture content for optimal quality.
Crush the roots into fine powder using a mill or mortar with 30 minutes of processing time.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of distilled water.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler with 30 minutes of preparation time.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's warming and calming effects can also help to neutralize excess acid production in the stomach, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion symptoms associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and acid production in the stomach, reducing acidity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cinnamaldehyde content in Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and bloating, by inhibiting the release of inflammatory mediators.

remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This phenolic compound is responsible for the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hyperacidity by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and protecting against oxidative stress.

Eugenol: As a phenolic compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, eugenol helps counteract hyperacidity by reducing inflammation in the stomach, alleviating pain, and preventing the growth of harmful bacteria.

remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Cinnamomum verum are used to make capsules for hyperacidity because they contain cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Cinnamomum verum are used to make capsules for hyperacidity because they are rich in tannins, which have a soothing effect on the stomach and help to reduce acidity.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinnamomum verum are used to make capsules for hyperacidity because they contain cinnamaldehyde and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Collect 500g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from a reputable source.
Grind the cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 250g of the ground cinnamomum verum powder with 750g of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 1000ml of boiling water to the gelatin mixture and stir until dissolved in 10 minutes.
Allow the gelatin mixture to cool and set in a refrigerator for 2 hours before filling capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with hyperacidity because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, particularly curcumin. This bioactive compound has been shown to neutralize excess stomach acid, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the mucous membranes in the stomach lining from damage caused by acidic secretions, promoting a healthy digestive environment and alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity. Regular consumption of turmeric capsules can bring relief from frequent heartburn episodes and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Citrinin: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori (H. pylori) bacteria that can contribute to hyperacidity by producing excess stomach acid.

Tumerones: Contains bioactive compounds that have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, helping to soothe and heal the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and neutralize free radicals in the stomach, thereby alleviating hyperacidity symptoms.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This curcuminoid has been shown to inhibit the production of gastric acid and pepsin, enzymes responsible for acid production in the stomach, thereby reducing hyperacidity.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): Similar to DMC, BDMC has been found to reduce gastric acid production and inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori bacteria, which can contribute to hyperacidity and stomach ulcers.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa for hyperacidity as they contain a high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used for hyperacidity as they contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues.

Barks: Barks of Curcuma longa are occasionally used for hyperacidity, although less commonly than rhyzomes or roots, as they contain some amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that may provide relief from digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Grind 200 grams of dried curcuma longa root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Sift 2 cups of gelatin through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove lumps.
Combine 1 cup of gelatin with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and heat on low for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground curcuma longa powder with 1 cup of the warm gelatin mixture for 30 seconds.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the curcuma longa-infused gelatin mixture using a small spoon for 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Crush 2 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the crushed root with 2 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 3 minutes.
Gradually add 10 ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40°c for 10 minutes to dissolve gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve freshness.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of dried commiphora mukul resin and mix it with 100g of herbal powder in a large bowl.

Use a capsule machine to fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the herbal commiphora mukul capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 24 months.

Mix 1g of the powdered mentha x piperita with 0.5g of a natural capsule filler to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the mentha x piperita blend and seal immediately to prevent moisture absorption.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for hyperacidity are natural supplements containing a blend of herbs that help alleviate symptoms of excessive acid production in the stomach, reducing heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. The best herbal capsules for hyperacidity include Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe vera, Foeniculum vulgare, Terminalia chebula, Mentha x piperita, Piper nigrum, Trigonella foenum-graecum, and Centella asiatica. If left untreated, hyperacidity can lead to digestive problems, weight loss, and even affect daily life, causing individuals to miss work or social events due to persistent discomfort and pain.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperacidity that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive disorders such as bloating, gas, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal supplements like peppermint, ginger, and fennel help alleviate these symptoms by soothing the stomach lining, reducing inflammation, and promoting regular digestion. These herbs can also treat conditions like dyspepsia, acid reflux, and stomach ulcers, providing a natural alternative to conventional treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with hyperacidity is a blend of Dandelion, Licorice root, and Amla. Dandelion helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn, while Licorice root soothes and protects the stomach lining. Amla, rich in antioxidants, promotes digestive balance and reduces acidity. Taking these capsules together can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as bloating, discomfort, and acid reflux. Consult a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements to determine the best dosage and combination for your specific needs.

Wash the roots under running water for 5 minutes to remove dirt and debris.
Slice the roots into thin pieces using a sharp knife or a root slicer for 10 minutes.
Dry the sliced roots in a single layer at 40°c for 3 hours using a food dehydrator.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of dried zingiber officinale powder for each capsule.
